pr.title: itlikelike cleanup with inlines, add itemstatetable
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up src/melee/it/items/itlikelike.c by adding the Like Like ItemStateTable, introducing local inline helpers for repeated velocity/state-transition logic, and refining the Like Like attributes header so x0 can be read as either Vec3* or S32Vec3*. The PR improved matching for Like Like data and several functions, including full .data and .sdata2 matches, while causing two 1-byte regressions reported by the bot.
what_changed: Added const Vec3 zero_vec = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f } and used it in it_802DC4BC instead of the previous global Vec3 it_803B86D8.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F8468[] for Like Like, mapping motion IDs to itLikelike_UnkMotion*_Anim, *_Phys, and *_Coll callbacks, including NULL for the motion 18 collision callback.
Changed itLikelikeAttributes field x0 in itCommonItems.h from a plain Vec3* to a union containing Vec3* x0_f32 and S32Vec3* x0_s32.
Updated Like Like attribute accesses from attr->x0->y to attr->x0.x0_f32->y where the data is treated as floating-point vector data.
Updated damage threshold logic in it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived to compare ip->xC9C against attr->x0.x0_s32->x instead of attr->x4.
Changed the random branch in it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived from pointer arithmetic *(it_804D6D40 + 4) to indexed access it_804D6D40[2].
Added local inline helpers itSwapVelocity, swapVelocity, and likelikeVelocity to express repeated Like Like velocity reversal and low-movement stall handling.
Replaced repeated reset/transition sequences with calls to existing helpers such as it_802D9DDC, it_802DA960, it_802DA104, it_802DAA10, and it_802DAD18.
Simplified several functions by using GET_ITEM(gobj), direct decrement/update forms, HSD_GObjEvent casting, and helper calls instead of duplicated temporary-variable-heavy code.
Cleaned up it_802DAD18 to use GET_ITEM(gobj), PAD_STACK(0x20), and HSD_JObjSetRotationZ(GET_JOBJ(gobj), M_PI).
smart_moves: Moved the Like Like state table into C as ItemStateTable it_803F8468, producing a bot-reported 100% .data match for main/melee/it/items/itlikelike.
Used a union for itLikelikeAttributes.x0 to document and match the fact that the same attribute slot is interpreted as both Vec3* and S32Vec3* in different code paths.
Kept itSwapVelocity local rather than moving it to inlines.h, with an explicit comment that dynamic GET_ATTRS prevents a generic inline-header version.
Factored repeated velocity and low-movement checks into likelikeVelocity while preserving the matching-sensitive structure enough to finish itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Replaced long repeated transition sequences with calls to known helper functions, making the code shorter and easier to audit while retaining or improving matches in many places.
Used zero_vec as a named const vector, which helped expose .sdata2/.rodata data and cleaned up item spawn logic in it_802DC4BC.
decomp_lessons: When an item attribute field is reused with different scalar interpretations, a small union in the shared header can be cleaner and more match-friendly than forcing one pointer type everywhere.
Item state tables are valuable data-matching targets: once function callbacks and state IDs are identified, adding the table can convert an entire .data section from unmatched to matched.
Local static inline wrappers can reduce duplicated code and affect register allocation favorably, but should stay local when they depend on file-specific macros like GET_ATTRS.
Existing transition helpers should be reused once identified; replacing repeated call sequences with the helper can improve readability and sometimes matching.
Small expression-shape changes matter for decomp matching: examples in this PR include array indexing versus pointer arithmetic, GET_ITEM versus HSD_GObjGetUserData, and direct compound assignment.
PAD_STACK remains a useful tactic for matching stack layout after cleanup, as shown in it_802DAD18.
Bot reports can show net progress even when tiny regressions remain; here the PR gained five new matches but broke two previously 100% functions by one byte each.
naming_conventions: Like Like motion callbacks follow the pattern itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Anim, itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itLikelike_UnkMotionN_Coll.
The newly added state table uses the existing data-symbol style it_803F8468 rather than a descriptive name.
The zero vector was named zero_vec, replacing the less-informative global data symbol it_803B86D8.
The attribute union keeps the original offset name x0 and names interpretations by scalar type: x0_f32 for Vec3* and x0_s32 for S32Vec3*.
New local helper names are descriptive but scoped to the file: itSwapVelocity, swapVelocity, and likelikeVelocity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added ItemStateTable it_803F8468 to match Like Like .data, producing a bot-reported +392 bytes and 100.00% .data for the unit.
Used a const zero Vec3 to expose and improve small data/rodata matching around it_802DC4BC and .sdata2.
Applied PAD_STACK(0x20) in it_802DAD18 after removing an artificial do/while stack-padding construct.
Kept a matching-only empty conditional in itLikelike_UnkMotion16_Anim: if (attr->x0.x0_f32->y && attr->x0.x0_f32->y) {} with a comment that it is for 100%.
Changed an event callback cast to (HSD_GObjEvent) it_802D9B78 in itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll.
Reduced register-temp noise by using GET_ITEM(gobj), helper calls, and direct gobj references instead of extra Item_GObj* and Item* temporaries.
Bot-reported new full matches: .data, it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived, .sdata2, it_802DAD18, and itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp-dev bot reported matched code 54.49% (+0.01%, +360 bytes) and matched data 34.51% (+0.04%, +440 bytes).
decomp-dev bot reported 5 new matches: .data, it_2725_Logic5_DmgReceived, .sdata2, it_802DAD18, and itLikelike_UnkMotion1_Coll.
decomp-dev bot reported 2 broken matches by 1 byte each: it_2725_Logic5_Dropped dropped to 99.67% and itLikelike_UnkMotion6_Phys dropped to 99.80%.
follow_up_queries: Check whether the two 1-byte regressions in it_2725_Logic5_Dropped and itLikelike_UnkMotion6_Phys were fixed in later Like Like PRs.
Search for other item attribute structs where a pointer field should be represented as a union of Vec3* and S32Vec3* interpretations.
Look for remaining item .data sections missing ItemStateTable definitions that can be recovered using this pattern.
Audit repeated item reset/transition call sequences that can be replaced by known helpers like it_802D9DDC or item-specific transition helpers.
Search for remaining placeholder zero-vector globals that could be replaced with named const Vec3 zero_vec-style objects.
